source_pr.title: bindinfo: fix unstable test
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

```
FAIL: bind_test.go:227: testSuite.TestSessionBinding

bind_test.go:261:
    c.Check(chk.NumRows(), Equals, 0)
... obtained int = 1
... expected int = 0
```
### What is changed and how it works?

The timeline is as follows:

- In the previous test `TestGlobalBinding`, it adds one global binding.
- Since the `Lease` is 3s, there will be a background goroutine calls `Update`, and it has successfully read the added binding by SQL.
- `TestGlobalBinding` drops the bindings, and the binding is also removed from cache, so the result of `show global bindings` is 0.
- In background goroutine, `Update` will update the cache using the SQL result, which is already outdated, so the result of `show global bindings` in `TestSessionBinding` becomes 1.

This PR fixes it by setting `Lease` to 0 so there will be no background goroutines.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - No code

Code changes

 - None

Side effects

 - None

Related changes

 - None

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because bindinfo updates its global binding cache asynchronously (background update goroutine controlled by `bindinfo.Lease`). A previous test can drop bindings but the background update can repopulate the cache with stale SQL results, causing the next test to see unexpected bindings. This PR stabilizes the test by disabling the background updater in tests (`bindinfo.Lease = 0`).
fix_pattern: Disable bindinfo background updates in tests by setting `bindinfo.Lease = 0` to avoid stale async cache refresh racing with test cleanup.
